title: Marbella Boat Rental 2026: Prices, Guide & Best Charters
html:
If you are planning a day on the water along the Costa del Sol, renting a boat from Puerto Banús in Marbella offers access to the Mediterranean Sea, with prices starting from around €400 for a half-day motorboat. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators and a range of vessels to suit your group and budget.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Prices for boat rental in Marbella range from €300 for a shared tour to €2,500 and above for a full-day crewed yacht. The total depends on the boat size, season, and whether you choose a skippered or bareboat option. For a mid-range experience, a 30ft motorboat for a half-day in high season typically costs around €600 to €900. You can browse skippered charters in Marbella on BoatHire24 to compare verified listings and check availability for your dates.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most charters depart from Puerto Banús, the luxury marina in the west of Marbella. Other departure points include the older Puerto Deportivo de Marbella in the town centre, the small harbour at Cabopino, and the nearby port of Estepona to the south-west. From any of these, you can head east towards the rocky coastline of La Cala de Mijares or west to the exclusive enclave of Sotogrande. Popular anchorages include the bay of Puerto Banús and the isolated coves near Cabo Pino lighthouse.

Which boat to rent

Choose a boat type based on your group size, desired experience, and budget. Here are four popular options for Marbella:

  1. Sunseeker Predator 72, a luxury performance motor yacht for up to 12 guests, from €2,200 for a full day. For details, visit the Sunseeker Predator 72 charter page.
  2. Azimut 48, a stylish flybridge motor cruiser for 10 guests, from €1,400 per day.
  3. Lagoon 450 catamaran, stable and spacious for 10 guests, from €1,800 for a full-day charter.
  4. Axopar 28, a fast, open day boat for up to 8 guests, from €500 for a half-day.

What are the peak seasons and typical winds?

The high season for Marbella boat rental runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind from the west is most common. This offshore breeze can pick up in the afternoon, making early morning departures calmer. Spring and autumn offer pleasant conditions with fewer crowds. Winter charters are possible but less frequent due to cooler sea temperatures and occasional Levante winds. Always check the latest marine forecast before setting out.

For a calmer, more sheltered experience, head to the bay of Estepona, where the coastline provides natural protection from the Poniente wind.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

In Spain, anyone born after 1988 must hold a valid Patrón de Navegación Básica licence to drive a powerboat over 8 metres or with more than 15hp. However, many smaller boats up to 8 metres can be rented without a licence on a day-trip basis. Skippered charters require no licence from you, as the captain takes full responsibility. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator, as regulations can vary by vessel and location.

Cost breakdown for Marbella boat rental

Prices vary by boat size, duration, and level of service. A typical price band breakdown in 2026:

  • Shared tour (RIB or small catamaran) per person: €60 to €90
  • Small motorboat (up to 22ft) half-day: €300 to €550
  • Catamaran (up to 40ft) full-day for up to 10 guests: €1,200 to €1,800
  • Crewed luxury motor yacht (50ft+) full-day: from €2,000 to €3,500

At a glance

  • Price range: €300 to €3,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Maximum guests: 12 on most boats
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a licence?

Yes, you can rent most small motorboats up to 8 metres without a licence, or choose a skippered charter where a captain handles navigation.

What is the best time of day to go boating in Marbella?

Morning departures between 9am and 12pm are recommended for calm seas and to enjoy the full eight-hour trip before the afternoon wind picks up.

How far is it from Puerto Banús to Marbella town by boat?

The distance by sea from Puerto Banús to Marbella town's Puerto Deportivo is approximately 4 nautical miles east along the coast.

Can I bring my own food and drink on a chartered boat?

Most charter companies allow you to bring your own supplies, but some provide catering packages; check with the operator when you book.

Whether you are looking for a skipper‑led luxury excursion or a self‑drive day boat, BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. To explore all options, visit the BoatHire24 marketplace and browse hundreds of verified listings.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
